definition: The action or fact of persuading someone or of being persuaded to do or believe something
example: The power of speech is the chief distinction between humans and animals, and the most successful means of achieving human cooperation.

definition: The action or process of working together to the same end
example: The power of speech is the chief distinction between humans and animals, and the most successful means of achieving human cooperation.

definition: The process of receiving or giving systematic instruction
example: The true test of education is not what a man knows, but what he does with his knowledge and how he uses his speech for the good of the city.

definition: The quality of being honest and having strong moral principles
example: We must not judge a man by his words alone, but by whether his actions are in harmony with his speech.

definition: The combination of simultaneously sounded musical notes to produce a pleasing effect
example: We must not judge a man by his words alone, but by whether his actions are in harmony with his speech.

definition: A person who gives advice or guidance
example: He who is a good counselor to his friends and a wise guide to his city deserves greater honor than the man who wins many battles.
speech: To Nicocles (letter of advice to a ruler)

definition: Behavior showing high moral standards
example: A good orator must first be a good man; no amount of technical skill can compensate for a corrupt character.

definition: The mental and moral qualities distinctive to an individual
example: A good orator must first be a good man; no amount of technical skill can compensate for a corrupt character.

definition: Cause to act dishonestly in return for money or personal gain
example: A good orator must first be a good man; no amount of technical skill can compensate for a corrupt character.

definition: Just behavior or treatment
example: The man who can speak most eloquently on behalf of justice will be the true benefactor of his fellow citizens.

definition: In a fluent or persuasive manner
example: The man who can speak most eloquently on behalf of justice will be the true benefactor of his fellow citizens.

definition: A person who gives money or other help to a person or cause
example: The man who can speak most eloquently on behalf of justice will be the true benefactor of his fellow citizens.

definition: The rearing and education of a child; cultural upbringing
example: Education is the best provision for old age; it furnishes a man with a treasure that time cannot take away.

definition: A quantity of precious metals, gems, or other valuable objects
example: Education is the best provision for old age; it furnishes a man with a treasure that time cannot take away.

definition: A condition or circumstance that puts one in a favorable or superior position
example: We must not allow the desire for immediate advantage to lead us into actions that will bring lasting disgrace.

definition: Loss of reputation or respect as the result of a dishonorable action
example: We must not allow the desire for immediate advantage to lead us into actions that will bring lasting disgrace.

definition: A principle of order and knowledge
example: Speech is the ruler and king of all things; with it we can make the false appear true and the true appear false.

definition: Control of one's emotions, desires, or actions by one's own will
example: The man who has learned to govern himself is fit to govern others; the man who cannot control his own tongue is fit for nothing.
speech: To Nicocles (letter of advice to a ruler)

definition: Concerned with the principles of right and wrong behavior
example: A good orator must first be a good man; no amount of technical skill can compensate for a corrupt character.

definition: The use of clever but false arguments, especially with the intention of deceiving
example: He emphasized moral education and ethical persuasion over the sophistry of his contemporaries.
